sprint: W1c Ã— M-Z partition bridge analysis
verdict_qualifier: All three W1c sub-layers classify cleanly under the M-Z partition (cross-shift / endomorphism). Sub-layer 3 admits a sub-classification (basis-closable cross-shift) that the original two-bucket M-Z partition does not name, which is genuinely new content.

A_L.description: H-centered multiplication algebra acting on H-localized orbitals
A_L.concrete_realization: C_0(R^3_H) restricted to H sub-block, dimension at NaH max_n=2 = 5 (H-side states)
A_L.physical_role: Left action: H valence basis (hydrogenic Z=1)

A_R.description: Na-centered multiplication algebra acting on Na-valence orbitals with [Ne] frozen core
A_R.concrete_realization: C_0(R^3_Na) âŠ— FrozenCore(Z=11) compression, dimension at NaH max_n=2 = 5 (Na-side states)
A_R.physical_role: Right action: Na valence basis (Z_orb=1 hydrogenic placeholder OR multi-zeta physical fit)

M.concrete_realization: 10-dimensional space spanned by 5 H-side + 5 Na-side hydrogenic orbitals; 2-electron FCI dimension = C(10,1)^2 = 100
M.notes: Actions are genuinely two-sided in the M-Y / M-Z sense: A_L acts on H sub-block matrix elements, A_R acts on Na sub-block matrix elements. Cross-V_ne (multipole expansion) couples them as a bimodule cross-shift kernel; on-block ERIs are pure endomorphisms of one or the other module.

sub_layer_1_PK_orthogonality.mechanism: Phillips-Kleinman cross-center barrier Î”H^PK_pq = Î£_c (E_v - E_c) S_pc S_cq
sub_layer_1_PK_orthogonality.justification: PK kernel is bilinear: left-action on H valence orbital p (via cross-overlap S_pc) and right-action on Na core orbital c (via E_v - E_c weighting). The integrand mixes the H-localized valence with Na-localized core wavefunctions through a coordinate-mediated overlap, which is the canonical signature of a bimodule cross-shift in the LatrÃ©moliÃ¨re framework.
sub_layer_1_PK_orthogonality.empirical_match: 14.6% W1c-descent reduction at NaH max_n=2 (F1-P2 Â§3.2); consistent with framework's bimodule machinery providing legitimate Layer 2 content.
sub_layer_1_PK_orthogonality.consistency_with_M_Z: MATCHES â€” M-Z predicts cross-shifts are handled by the framework's bimodule machinery; PK is implemented and produces measurable physical content.

sub_layer_2_multi_zeta_Na_shape.mechanism: Multi-zeta substitution of Na 3s/3p radial wavefunctions: hydrogenic-Z=1 placeholder replaced by FrozenCore-screened physical Na valence (overlap > 0.999999 at K=5 mixed-n)
sub_layer_2_multi_zeta_Na_shape.justification: Multi-zeta changes the radial shape WITHIN the Na sub-module (A_R-acting space) WITHOUT cross-coupling to the H sub-module (A_L-acting space). The substitution is a self-deformation of the right-action module's basis functions; the bimodule cross-shift structure (cross-V_ne) is then composed onto the deformed module shape. The endomorphism content alone does NOT couple to H.
sub_layer_2_multi_zeta_Na_shape.framework_handles_qualifier: PARTIALLY â€” the endomorphism content (Na shape) is structurally external input (from FrozenCore solver + Clementi-Roetti fits), not derivable from the framework's bimodule machinery. BUT the cross-V_ne bimodule cross-shift kernel composes the endomorphism content into observable matrix elements cleanly.
sub_layer_2_multi_zeta_Na_shape.empirical_match: Bit-zero on FCI without W1c (Î±-PES Step 2); load-bearing under W1c (23% additional W1c-descent reduction, F1-P2 Â§3.2). The mutual-exclusivity behavior is the structural signature: an endomorphism on an unoccupied module is invisible; once cross-shift activates that module's occupation, the endomorphism becomes visible.
sub_layer_2_multi_zeta_Na_shape.consistency_with_M_Z: MATCHES â€” M-Z predicts endomorphisms are NOT generated by bimodule machinery. Multi-zeta exponents come from external Clementi-Roetti fits, consistent with the LS-8a-class wall (renormalization counterterms come from external UV-completion). Chemistry-side analog of LS-8a wall but MITIGABLE: external input is computable (FrozenCore solver), unlike LS-8a counterterms which require a UV-completion theory.

sub_layer_3_orbital_pair_flexibility.mechanism: FCI lacks bonding-vs-antibonding orbital pair construction at NaH max_n=2 with W1c+mz; natural occupations are [1,1] = two separately-occupied orbitals (one Na 3s, one H 1s), NOT bonding/antibonding partition with one mixed pair occupied
sub_layer_3_orbital_pair_flexibility.justification: Bonding/antibonding combinations [H 1s + Na 3s]_Â± are explicitly BIMODULE CROSS-SHIFT linear combinations (sum of left-action and right-action basis elements with specific sign coefficients). At max_n=2 the basis has H 1s and Na 3s as separate basis functions but lacks dimensional richness for the FCI to construct +/- combinations with energy lower than the separated-orbital configuration. At larger max_n the additional cross-shift dimensions (H 2s/2p, Na 3p/3d) provide the orbital-mixing flexibility the FCI needs. CLASS is cross-shift (the linear combination is bimodule-structural); OBSTRUCTION is basis dimensionality, not endomorphism content.
sub_layer_3_orbital_pair_flexibility.framework_handles_qualifier: BASIS-SIZE-LIMITED â€” the cross-shift mechanism exists in the framework, but the basis must be rich enough at the truncation level for the FCI to realize it. max_n=2 is too sparse; max_n=3 should provide the additional dimensions.
sub_layer_3_orbital_pair_flexibility.empirical_match: PES monotonically descending at NaH max_n=2 with W1c+mz (F1-P2 Â§3.2); natural occupations [1.000, 1.000] for the two near-degenerate orbitals (F1-P2 Â§4.1). The missing bonding combination is structurally a cross-shift, not an endomorphism.
sub_layer_3_orbital_pair_flexibility.consistency_with_M_Z: PARTIAL MATCH with sub-classification â€” the original M-Z partition has only two buckets (handled cross-shift / not-handled endomorphism). Sub-layer 3 introduces a third sub-classification: basis-closable cross-shift, where the mechanism IS a cross-shift but the basis truncation prevents its FCI realization. This is a refinement of M-Z that the analysis could not have anticipated from the bound-state QED side, but emerges naturally from FCI variational state analysis on the chemistry side.

prediction_1.label: P1: Internal PES minimum emerges
prediction_1.statement: NaH F1 max_n=3 with combined W1c + multi-zeta architecture produces an internal PES minimum at R_eq âˆˆ [3.0, 4.5] bohr (experimental NaH R_eq = 3.566 bohr).
prediction_1.falsifiable_threshold: If R_min sits at the smallest tested R value (e.g., R=2.0 bohr) at max_n=3, the prediction is REFUTED.
prediction_1.underlying_classification: Sub-layer 3 = basis-closable cross-shift; max_n=3 provides the dimensional richness for bonding orbital construction.
prediction_1.confidence_rationale: Depends on the validity of the basis-closable hypothesis. Alternative interpretation: max_n=3 also provides more diffuse orbitals on H-side that further dominate the bonding manifold, leaving the wall unchanged. CLAUDE.md Â§2 NaH n_max=3 v2.1.1 was tested at BARE balanced and overattracted; this prediction tests at W1c-screened + multi-zeta which is structurally different.

prediction_2.label: P2: Binding energy within 2Ã— of experimental
prediction_2.statement: If P1 holds (internal minimum exists), the binding energy D_e at the internal minimum is within a factor 2 of experimental NaH D_e â‰ˆ 0.075 Ha (i.e., D_e âˆˆ [0.0375, 0.150] Ha).
prediction_2.falsifiable_threshold: If D_e (internal minimum to dissociation) is outside [0.0375, 0.150] Ha, the prediction is REFUTED. A D_e < 0.0375 Ha indicates incomplete bonding mechanism; D_e > 0.150 Ha indicates residual over-attraction.
prediction_2.underlying_classification: Sub-layer 2 endomorphism content (multi-zeta) is mitigable via FrozenCore input; under combined architecture should give physical-scale energetics.
prediction_2.confidence_rationale: Even if P1 holds, the absolute D_e value depends on the cross-V_ne kernel-shape accuracy (Track 3's named target P2 which is NOT addressed by this sprint). A factor-2 tolerance is conservative; tighter agreement would suggest the cross-V_ne kernel is also adequate, which is a separate question.

prediction_3.label: P3: Multi-zeta differential persists at max_n=3 with magnitude similar to max_n=2
prediction_3.statement: The W1c+mz vs W1c-alone FCI energy differential at R_eq is in the range [0.02, 0.30] Ha (similar to max_n=2's +0.056 Ha at R=3.5).
prediction_3.falsifiable_threshold: If |E(W1c+mz) - E(W1c)| at R_eq < 0.005 Ha at max_n=3, multi-zeta has been absorbed by basis expansion and is no longer load-bearing (cross-shift content from W1c is dominant; endomorphism content from mz becomes structural noise). If |E(W1c+mz) - E(W1c)| > 0.30 Ha, the differential is growing with basis size, suggesting the endomorphism content scales non-trivially with basis â€” would indicate the multi-zeta architecture has unexpected basis-set dependence.
prediction_3.underlying_classification: Sub-layer 2 endomorphism content is fixed (Na shape is what it is); cross-shift content (cross-V_ne) provides the matrix-element visibility. Larger basis adds more cross-V_ne couplings, but the per-cell endomorphism content is bounded.
prediction_3.confidence_rationale: The endomorphism-vs-cross-shift partition predicts this composition behavior cleanly. If P3 is violated in either direction, the structural classification of multi-zeta as endomorphism would need revision.

qed_side_endomorphism.structural_reason: Counterterms parameterize divergent loop integrals that the framework's spectral action cannot regulate autonomously (LS-8a memo). The UV cutoff and the counterterm values come from a deeper UV-completion structure outside the spectral triple.

examples: Cross-V_ne kernel shape on heavy-atom partner
chemistry_side_endomorphism.external_input_type: FrozenCore radial SchrÃ¶dinger eigenstates + Clementi-Raimondi / Clementi-Roetti / BBB93 atomic basis fits
chemistry_side_endomorphism.structural_reason: Chemistry endomorphism content is the atomic-structure data of the frozen-core system, which is computable autonomously by atomic-physics methods (HF, multi-zeta fits to RHF orbitals). The framework's molecular bimodule machinery composes pre-computed atomic endomorphism content into molecular cross-shift structures.
chemistry_side_endomorphism.structural_argument: QED-side renormalization is open-ended because the SM is not UV-complete (asymptotic freedom of QED is not proven; the Landau pole is a real feature). Chemistry-side atomic structure is closed-form computable to arbitrary precision via well-posed eigenvalue problems on FrozenCore. The distinction is between an open-ended UV problem (QED) and a closed-form atomic eigenvalue problem (chemistry). This is structural, not contingent on current technology.

sprint_sequence: Step 1 (HIGH PRIORITY, 3-5 days): F1 max_n=3 NaH FCI with W1c + multi-zeta architecture. Tests predictions P1, P2, P3 simultaneously. Falsifiable, structured.

Step 2 (CONDITIONAL on P1 holding): If max_n=3 gives binding, write up M-Z partition as a general framework tool in Paper 19 Â§sec:w1c_residual + Paper 34 Â§V.D. The two-bucket â†’ three-bucket refinement (cross-shift / basis-closable cross-shift / endomorphism) is the genuinely new structural content.

Step 3 (CONDITIONAL on P1 failing): If max_n=3 does NOT give binding, sub-layer 3 is genuinely an endomorphism class (basis cannot generate the bonding combination from within). Pivot to Track 3's named target P2 (cross-V_ne kernel-shape substitution), which addresses a distinct cross-shift mechanism.

documentation_promotion.rationale: If F1 max_n=3 confirms P1 (binding emerges via basis-closable cross-shift), the M-Z partition has predicted a specific physics outcome on a system class (alkali hydrides) it was not originally designed for. This would justify promoting it as a general framework tool. If F1 max_n=3 refutes P1, the partition's predictive power is limited to the QED-side bound-state work where it was originally derived; promote it more conservatively as a local-vocabulary refinement.

do_not_apply_in_this_sprint: No edits to CLAUDE.md, Paper 34, Paper 19 (let PI decide based on F1 outcome)
No modifications to production code (geovac/balanced_coupled.py, etc.) â€” this is structural analysis only
No new sub-agent dispatches â€” keep the analysis self-contained

what_is_proven_at_structural_level: Bimodule structure (A_L, A_R, M) for NaH balanced is well-defined and matches LatrÃ©moliÃ¨re framework
Sub-layer 1 (PK) is unambiguously a bimodule cross-shift by construction
Sub-layer 2 (multi-zeta) is unambiguously a module endomorphism by construction
Sub-layer 3 (orbital-pair flexibility) admits a cross-shift classification with basis-closable qualifier
what_is_structural_sketch: Predictions P1, P2, P3 for F1 max_n=3 â€” based on classification reasoning, not on direct computation
The structural-vs-contingent argument for chemistry endomorphism mitigability â€” defensible but not a theorem
The basis-closable sub-classification of cross-shifts â€” emergent from F1 analysis, not from M-Z's original framework
what_is_open: Whether F1 max_n=3 actually closes the wall (P1 prediction is the test)
Whether the basis-closable sub-classification generalizes beyond NaH (test on KH, RbH, CsH)
Whether the M-Z partition extends to other chemistry walls (cross-V_ne kernel shape, lone pair coupling at Hâ‚‚O Z_eff>4, etc.)
Whether the LS-8a-analog reading sharpens at multi-loop higher orders (Track M-Z.2 named follow-on)

summary_one_line: Three W1c sub-layers classify as: (1) bimodule cross-shift, (2) module endomorphism, (3) basis-closable cross-shift. M-Z partition extends cleanly with one sub-classification refinement. F1 max_n=3 tests three falsifiable predictions; PI-decision on documentation promotion gated on outcome.

verification.verdict_summary: P1 FAILED (R_min at smallest tested R=2.0 bohr, exactly the falsification criterion). P2 FAILED (conditional on P1). P3 PASSED (multi-zeta differential within predicted [0.02, 0.30] Ha range at R=2.0). The basis-closable cross-shift hypothesis for sub-layer 3 is REFUTED at max_n=3; sub-layer 3 reclassifies as a genuine endomorphism class (cannot be closed by basis enlargement at this scale).

prediction_1.predicted_range_bohr: NaH F1 max_n=3 with combined W1c + multi-zeta architecture produces an internal PES minimum at R_eq âˆˆ [3.0, 4.5] bohr (experimental NaH R_eq = 3.566 bohr).
prediction_1.detail: R_min = 2.0 bohr (smallest tested) at max_n=3 under combined W1c+multi-zeta architecture. PES monotonically descending across R in {2.0, 2.5, 3.0, 3.5, 4.0, 5.0, 7.0, 10.0}. NO internal equilibrium minimum.

prediction_2.detail: D_e (PES, smallest-R to dissociation) = +0.7097 Ha -- about 10x larger than the experimental NaH 0.075 Ha, indicating continued over-attraction at small R. This is the spurious-binding signature, not a real D_e.

prediction_3.detail: |E_W1c - E_W1c+mz| at R=R_min = 2.0 bohr is 0.2066 Ha, within the predicted [0.02, 0.30] range. Multi-zeta is load-bearing at max_n=3 (consistent with F1-P1+P2 finding at max_n=2). The endomorphism classification of multi-zeta is preserved across basis sizes.

structural_findings: CRITICAL POSITIVE: at max_n=3 the dominant natural orbital of combined W1c+mz at R=3.5 shows true bonding combination (Na 3s + H 1s, ~50/50 amplitude split with mixing coefficients -0.698 Na 3s + -0.687 H 1s). At max_n=2 it was H-dominant (1.0 H amplitude). Larger basis DID enable bonding-combination construction. The 2nd natural orbital is the antibonding combination (-0.698 Na 3s + +0.687 H 1s).
BUT: natural occupations remain [1.0, 1.0] (not [2.0, 0.0]), so the FCI is still an open-shell singlet of two singly-occupied orbitals (bonding and antibonding), NOT a closed-shell bonded pair.
PES is monotonically descending across full range R in {2.0, ..., 10.0} bohr; no internal minimum at any tested R. PES descent depth = 0.71 Ha at R=2.0 vs R=10.0 (combined W1c+mz).
Multi-zeta differential R-dependence: smoothly decays from -0.207 Ha at R=2.0 to bit-zero at R=10.0 (same R-dependent pattern as F1-P1+P2 max_n=2).
W1c-alone PES descent depth at max_n=3: 0.916 Ha. Combined W1c+mz: 0.710 Ha. Multi-zeta reduces descent depth by ~22% at max_n=3, similar to ~23% at max_n=2.
Sub-layer 3 RECLASSIFIES: NOT basis-closable cross-shift, but GENUINE ENDOMORPHISM CLASS that the framework cannot close from inside its bimodule machinery.

sub_layer_3_reclassification.justification: Sprint F1 max_n=3 tested whether the basis enlargement from max_n=2 to max_n=3 (Q=20 to Q=56) provides the dimensional richness for the FCI to construct a true bonding combination with energy lower than the separated configuration. The result is mixed: the FCI DID construct a true bonding combination as the dominant natural orbital (Na 3s + H 1s, 50/50 amplitude split at R=3.5), but the bonding combination still has higher energy than the separated orbital configuration at every tested R. The PES is still monotonically descending; no internal minimum emerges. The basis enlargement closed the STRUCTURAL gap (bonding orbital is now constructible) but the ENERGETIC gap remains. Sub-layer 3 is therefore not basis-closable in the sense of producing binding-with-equilibrium; it requires a deeper closure mechanism beyond what the framework can generate from within its bimodule machinery at the tested scales.
sub_layer_3_reclassification.updated_taxonomy: 2-bucket M-Z partition unchanged: cross-shift (handled by framework, sub-layers 1 + sub-layer 3 substructure) vs endomorphism (external input, sub-layers 2 + reclassified sub-layer 3). Sub-layer 3 was originally hypothesized as basis-closable cross-shift; F1 max_n=3 falsifies this and confirms sub-layer 3 lives in the same endomorphism class as sub-layer 2 in the sense of being external to framework-side closure.
sub_layer_3_reclassification.consistency_with_M_Z_original: STRONG -- the M-Z 2-bucket partition (cross-shift / endomorphism) holds; the proposed 3-bucket refinement (cross-shift / basis-closable cross-shift / endomorphism) is not supported. The original W3 and LS-8a wall framing extends cleanly: there is calibration data the framework needs that it cannot generate from inside, even at modest basis enlargement.
sub_layer_3_reclassification.orbital_structure_at_max_n_3: At max_n=3 the framework constructs the right bonding orbital (structural success) but cannot lower its energy below the separated configuration (energetic failure). The two-singly-occupied natural occupation pattern [1.0, 1.0] persists from max_n=2 even though the orbital shapes change. This is a deeper finding than just "basis too small" -- it indicates the framework needs additional content (Track 3 P2: cross-V_ne kernel shape, or a structurally different solver) that is not accessible via basis enlargement on the current architecture.

next_sprint_recommendations: Apply paper edits per CLEAN NEGATIVE branch: Paper 19 sec:w1c_residual extension documenting the sub-layer 3 reclassification; CLAUDE.md §3 dead ends row for basis-closable hypothesis falsification.

Pivot to Track 3 named target P2 (cross-V_ne kernel-shape substitution): replace the bare cross-V_ne kernel integration on Na valence with physical-n hydrogenic shape (from FrozenCore solver) rather than just diagonal eigenvalue substitution. This addresses a structurally distinct mechanism from the orbital-pair flexibility tested here.
Alternative: pivot to a different system class (LiH+, HCl, MgH2) where the W1c-residual wall is empirically smaller and a partial closure may give physical binding even without complete W1c removal.

Do NOT pursue further max_n enlargement on NaH: at max_n=3 the dominant bonding orbital is already constructible, so the structural gap is closed at this basis. Further enlargement would not change the wall character.
